research_series.series_name: PSG/EEG Sleep Architecture Repair Tree: Sleep Stages, Slow-Wave Sleep, REM, Arousal, Sleep-Disordered Breathing, Fatigue, Cognition, Mood, and Wearable Discrepancy
research_series.repair_reason: PSG/EEG sleep architecture and sleep-disordered breathing were under-covered in previous cleaned trees.

core_interpretation: PSG/EEG-derived sleep architecture markers provide objective ground truth for sleep fragmentation, sleep stages, sleep-disordered breathing and wearable validation; total sleep time alone is insufficient to infer recovery.

result_variations.result_pattern: <7h TST or significant within-person reduction from baseline/control group
standalone_interpretation: Short TST alone does not define poor sleep quality or recovery.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Short TST plus next-day lapses supports acute sleep loss branch.

guardrails: Do not infer sleep recovery from total sleep time alone.

measured_entities.entity_name: Sleep Fragmentation: WASO, Arousal Index, Sleep Maintenance Efficiency

result_variations.result_pattern: High WASO and/or arousal index with normal or near-normal total sleep time
standalone_interpretation: Suggests disrupted sleep continuity; may impair recovery even when total sleep duration appears adequate.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: High fragmentation plus next-day impairment supports sleep fragmentation recovery failure branch.
Night noise plus arousal index plus next-day fatigue supports noise sleep recovery branch.

guardrails: Do not infer recovery from total sleep time alone.
Check pain, noise, CO2, temperature, medication and circadian phase.

measured_entities.entity_name: N3 / Slow-Wave Sleep / EEG Slow-Wave Activity

result_variations.result_pattern: Reduced N3 percent and/or reduced EEG slow-wave activity with normal or near-normal total sleep time
standalone_interpretation: May indicate impaired restorative sleep architecture; interpretation requires cognition/fatigue outcomes.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Low N3/SWA plus memory/fatigue supports sleep architecture recovery branch.

guardrails: Do not infer N3 impairment from consumer wearable staging.
Do not infer cognition mechanism without measured outcome.

measured_entities.entity_name: REM Percent, REM Latency, REM Fragmentation

result_variations.result_pattern: Reduced or fragmented REM sleep, abnormal REM latency, or REM shift relative to baseline
standalone_interpretation: REM changes are context-dependent and should not be treated as mood/cognition mechanism without linked outcomes.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: REM disruption plus mood change and circadian misalignment supports REM mood circadian branch.

guardrails: Do not infer mood mechanism from REM changes without measured mood/circadian outcomes.

measured_entities.entity_name: AHI, RDI, ODI, Minimum SpO2, Time Below 90%, Central/Obstructive Apnea Index

result_variations.result_pattern: Elevated AHI/RDI with elevated ODI and/or nocturnal oxygen desaturation
standalone_interpretation: Supports objective sleep-disordered breathing when linked to respiratory events; daytime SpO2 may remain normal.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: SDB markers plus daytime impairment supports sleep-disordered breathing branch.
Nocturnal desaturation with normal daytime SpO2 supports nocturnal respiratory sleep risk branch.

guardrails: Do not infer sleep apnea from fatigue or snoring alone without AHI/RDI/ODI.
Do not infer daytime oxygenation from nocturnal SpO2 alone.

result_variations.result_pattern: Elevated periodic limb movement index with arousals or sleep fragmentation
standalone_interpretation: May contribute to sleep fragmentation and daytime sleepiness when temporally linked.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: PLMI plus iron context may guide restless legs/limb movement interpretation.
PLMI plus fragmentation plus daytime sleepiness supports limb movement sleep fragmentation branch.

guardrails: Do not attribute daytime symptoms to limb movements without arousal/fragmentation and symptom timing.

measured_entities.entity_name: PSG vs Wearable Sleep Stage/Fragmentation Discrepancy

result_variations.result_pattern: Wearable shows normal sleep duration/stage distribution while PSG shows high arousals, low N3, or SDB
standalone_interpretation: Consumer wearable estimates can miss fragmentation, arousals, deep sleep loss or sleep-disordered breathing.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Normal wearable sleep with abnormal PSG supports wearable false reassurance branch.

guardrails: Do not infer PSG stages from consumer wearable staging.
Do not use consumer wearable staging to diagnose specific sleep disorders.

result_variations.result_pattern: Poor subjective sleep with normal PSG architecture and normal objective sleep continuity
standalone_interpretation: Suggests symptom perception, anxiety, pain, environmental or insomnia-cognitive context rather than objectively disrupted architecture.

cross_modal_links.combined_meaning: Poor subjective sleep with normal PSG should trigger context override search.

guardrails: Do not infer insomnia mechanism from subjective sleep complaint alone.

high_value_inference_chains.notes: Short objective sleep plus vigilance lapses and sleepiness supports acute sleep loss; TST alone is insufficient.
Fragmentation can predict impairment even when duration is preserved.
Reduced N3/SWS may impair memory/recovery, but outcome measurement is required.
Objective SDB plus daytime impairment supports sleep-disordered breathing branch.
Nocturnal oxygenation risk can be missed by daytime SpO2.
Wearables may miss arousals and microarchitecture abnormalities.
Subjective complaints may not match objective architecture.
Medication timing should be checked before inferring primary sleep pathology.

guardrails: Do not infer sleep recovery from total sleep time alone.
Do not infer PSG sleep stages from consumer wearable staging.
Do not infer sleep apnea from fatigue or snoring alone without AHI/RDI/ODI.
Do not infer daytime oxygenation from nocturnal SpO2 alone.
Do not infer mood or cognition mechanism from REM/N3 changes without measured outcomes.
Do not infer insomnia mechanism from subjective sleep complaint alone.
Do not ignore pain, medication, noise, CO2, circadian phase, and mission phase as context overrides.
Full-chain=true only if sleep architecture marker, mediator/context, and symptom/performance outcome are measured with timing.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Wearables can substitute for PSG in diagnosing specific sleep disorders or quantifying all sleep stages.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Systematic reviews/meta-analyses show wearables are less accurate for stage classification and fragmentation than PSG/EEG.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Total sleep time alone is sufficient to assess recovery or performance risk.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Fragmentation and deep-stage loss can predict impairment even when sleep duration is preserved.

research_gaps: Few studies simultaneously measure objective architecture, subjective quality, and next-day performance in the same individuals.
Limited validation of wearables for microarchitecture such as spindles, K-complexes and spectral power outside lab settings.
Sparse astronaut/spaceflight-specific PSG/EEG data on architecture-performance links.
Heterogeneity in reporting of microarchitecture and cross-modal outcomes across populations.

most_reliable_PSG_EEG_sleep_markers: WASO, arousal index and sleep maintenance efficiency for sleep continuity and cognitive/performance risk.
AHI/RDI/ODI/minimum nocturnal SpO2 for sleep-disordered breathing and nocturnal oxygenation risk.
N3/SWS and slow-wave activity for restorative architecture when linked to memory/fatigue outcomes.
most_context_dependent_markers: Total sleep time, which requires fragmentation and next-day outcome context.
Wearable-derived staging, which varies by device and is less reliable for arousals/deep stages.
Subjective sleep complaints, which may reflect pain, mood, environment, medication or perception mismatch.
highest_value_cross_modal_links: Short TST + PVT lapses + subjective sleepiness = acute sleep loss branch.
High WASO/arousal index + normal TST + next-day fatigue = fragmentation-driven impairment.
Wearable normal + PSG high arousals/low N3 = false reassurance risk.
High AHI/RDI/ODI + daytime sleepiness/fatigue = sleep-disordered breathing branch.
summary.overall_assessment: Objective PSG/EEG markers fill the missing parameter-level sleep architecture layer. Fragmentation and sleep microarchitecture often explain recovery/performance risk better than total duration alone. PSG/EEG remains the ground truth for stages, arousals and sleep-disordered breathing, while wearables are supportive but not diagnostic. This repair tree should be integrated as a context-sensitive objective sleep architecture layer rather than a standalone diagnosis module.
